title: T-008 — API: supplier CRUD + supplier_products assignment
prompt:
Implement supplier CRUD API per PRD §6.1 + FR-PLAN-001/002. Endpoints:
- GET /api/planning/suppliers (list, filter by is_active)
- GET /api/planning/suppliers/:id
- POST /api/planning/suppliers (Zod validate; country ISO 3166-1, currency ISO 4217, email regex, phone E.164 optional)
- PUT /api/planning/suppliers/:id
- DELETE /api/planning/suppliers/:id (soft delete: is_active=false)
- POST /api/planning/suppliers/:id/products (assign product with is_default flag, enforce max-1-default per product via T-001 partial-unique index)

Next.js Server Actions or route handlers acceptable; use Drizzle queries inside `withOrgContext(...)` so the foundation setter `app.set_org_context(session_token, org_id)` is invoked and RLS reads via `app.current_org_id()` (see `packages/db/migrations/002-rls-baseline.sql`). Do NOT write `app.tenant_id` GUC. Validation via Zod schemas in `src/lib/validators/supplier.ts`.

RED test: `src/app/api/planning/suppliers/__tests__/route.test.ts` covers V-PLAN-PO-001 (unique code per org), V-PLAN-PO-002 (max 1 default per product), soft-delete preserves history.

pipeline_inputs.description: RESTful CRUD for suppliers + supplier_products assignment with default enforcement; org scoping via foundation withOrgContext / app.set_org_context().
pipeline_inputs.details: Use Next.js App Router route handlers under `src/app/api/planning/suppliers/`. Org scoping via the foundation `withOrgContext(sessionToken, orgId, fn)` HOF (FT-001) which opens a tx and calls `app.set_org_context(session_token, org_id)`; RLS policies read `app.current_org_id()`. Do NOT set `app.tenant_id` GUC — it is spoofable and non-canonical. Zod validators export both `supplierCreateSchema` and `supplierUpdateSchema`. is_default conflict returns 409 with body { code: 'V-PLAN-PO-002', existing_default_supplier_id }. Soft-delete sets is_active=false; hard-delete forbidden (return 405). RLS uses foundation `app.current_org_id()` function (Wave0 decision; see `_meta/audits/2026-05-14-architecture-and-cross-cutting-gaps.md` §Tenant-context). AC consolidation: last two AC merged into one to satisfy module validator cap of 4 (Fixer F1, see `_meta/audits/2026-05-14-fixer-F1-tenant-and-foundation-citations.md`).
acceptance_criteria: Given two suppliers in same org with code='ACME', when second POST is attempted, then HTTP 409 is returned.
Given supplier_products row with is_default=true exists for product P, when another assignment with is_default=true is POSTed for same product, then HTTP 409 with code 'V-PLAN-PO-002'.
Given DELETE /api/planning/suppliers/:id is called, when fetched again, then row remains with is_active=false (soft delete).

Given a request is processed, when the route handler issues SQL, then it is wrapped in withOrgContext() so app.set_org_context() runs before any query and RLS reads app.current_org_id(); and `pnpm vitest run src/app/api/planning/suppliers/__tests__/route.test.ts`, when run, then 0 failures.
test_strategy: RED tests for V-PLAN-PO-001/002 + soft-delete semantics; tests assert that cross-org reads return 404 (RLS hides) and that no code path sets app.tenant_id GUC.
risk_red_lines: Do not read current_setting('app.tenant_id') or current_setting('app.current_org_id') directly — use the foundation app.current_org_id() function so the NULL-safe setter contract is preserved.
Do not hard-delete supplier rows — history retention required.
Do not skip Zod validation on the API boundary.
Do not allow cross-org reads — RLS via app.current_org_id() must be active for every request via withOrgContext().

cross_module_dependencies.reason: Consumes optional D365 integration adapter metadata; D365 never unlocks factory use.
